mission.title: Install and Configure Prometheus Operator / kube-prometheus-stack
mission.description: The Prometheus Operator simplifies the deployment and management of Prometheus monitoring stacks on Kubernetes. It allows users to configure Prometheus and Alertmanager using Kubernetes custom resources, making it easier to manage monitoring configurations. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes custom resources and potential complexity in advanced configurations.

steps.title: Add the Prometheus Operator Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Prometheus Operator Helm chart repository to your Helm client.

```bash

helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This repository contains the kube-prometheus-stack chart.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the kube-prometheus-stack
steps.description:
This command installs the kube-prometheus-stack Helm chart in the 'monitoring' namespace, which includes Prometheus, Alertmanager, and Grafana.

```bash

kubectl create namespace monitoring

helm install kube-prometheus-stack prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack --namespace monitoring --version 0.90.1

```

> Creates a namespace for the monitoring stack.
> Installs the kube-prometheus-stack with the specified version.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Prometheus and Alertmanager pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n monitoring

```

> Lists all pods in the monitoring namespace to verify their status.

steps.title: Access the Grafana dashboard
steps.description:
This command retrieves the Grafana admin password and provides instructions to access the dashboard.

```bash

kubectl get secret --namespace monitoring kube-prometheus-stack-grafana -o jsonpath="{.data.admin-password}" | base64 --decode

kubectl port-forward svc/kube-prometheus-stack-grafana -n monitoring 3000:80

```

> Retrieves the Grafana admin password.
> Forwards port 3000 to access Grafana locally.
commands: kubectl get secret --namespace monitoring kube-prometheus-stack-grafana -o jsonpath="{.data.admin-password}" | base64 --decode

uninstall.title: Uninstall the kube-prometheus-stack
uninstall.description:
This step removes the kube-prometheus-stack and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all monitoring data.

```bash

helm uninstall kube-prometheus-stack --namespace monitoring

kubectl delete namespace monitoring

```

> Uninstalls the Helm release.
> Deletes the monitoring namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the kube-prometheus-stack
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations. This step will upgrade the Helm release to the latest version.

```bash

helm get values kube-prometheus-stack --namespace monitoring > kube-prometheus-stack-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kube-prometheus-stack prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack --namespace monitoring --version 0.90.1

kubectl get pods -n monitoring

```

> Backs up the current configuration values.
> Upgrades the kube-prometheus-stack to the specified version.
> Verifies that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: helm get values kube-prometheus-stack --namespace monitoring > kube-prometheus-stack-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to recognize "": no matches for kind "Prometheus" in version "monitoring.coreos.com/v1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the Prometheus CRD is not installed or recognized.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the Prometheus Operator is installed correctly and the CRDs are applied.

troubleshooting.title: Grafana dashboard shows 'Data source not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Prometheus data source is not configured correctly in Grafana.

**Fix:**
Check the Grafana configuration and ensure that the Prometheus data source is set up correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Prometheus pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually happens due to misconfiguration or resource limits being exceeded.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Prometheus pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n monitoring' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Alertmanager is not sending alerts.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This could be due to misconfigured alert rules or notification channels.

**Fix:**
Review the Alertmanager configuration and ensure that the alert rules are correctly defined.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new alerting rules and improved Grafana dashboards.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old alerting configuration format.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.77 or earlier, migrate your alerting rules to the new format as per the documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see the Prometheus and Alertmanager pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n monitoring'.
codeSnippets: kubectl get secret --namespace monitoring kube-prometheus-stack-grafana -o jsonpath="{.data.admin-password}" | base64 --decode

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources.
